policy.proof_authority: Lean kernel and blocking CI only
policy.external_spend: Manual dispatch or a paths-scoped integration self-test only; never scheduled
policy.parked_experiments: Decision RS-2026-07-22-001 selected no route. Hypothesis-generation workflows remain manual and must not run until a superseding decision selects one route and both tasks/NEXT.md and experiments/HYPOTHESES.yaml explicitly authorize it
policy.merge: No workflow in this inventory is authorized to merge the architecture branch

workflows.reason: Blocking proof, trust, registry, and architecture verification.
Regenerates and drift-gates canonical derived artifacts.
Validates registered versioned corpus manifests, source relations, counts, hashes, and the experiment-authorization boundary.
Elaborates the blocked N7 stem when that stem changes.
Replays existing experiment correctness checks; does not authorize new hypotheses.
Manual warm Lean/CAS precheck; never a trust root.
No paid proof queue entries are authorized after the select-none route decision.
Draft-producing model work is deferred until a route or formal target is explicitly selected.
Discovery plus proof generation is outside monitored candidate intake after a select-none decision.
Formal frontier nodes are mapped but none is selected merely because it is open.
Model drafting is deferred; the decision layer assigns no active proof target.
All experimental hypotheses remain parked after RS-2026-07-22-001 selected no route.
New hypothesis generation is deferred until new evidence triggers a superseding route-selection decision.
Next-theorem proposals require a newly selected route rather than claim-count priority.
Useful only when changing the integration or its secret.
Self-test surface duplicates newer manual prover workflows.
No target is currently registered in the paid attempt queue.
